research_question: What is the geometric structure of the reward-contrastive embedding space?

conclusions.semantic_clustering: Weak across all axes. Player hand shows marginal positive clustering (silhouette=0.019); opponent hand shows no clustering (expected, as it is unobserved); round shows negligible clustering.
conclusions.dimensionality: 3 effective dimensions at 80% threshold. PCA dominated by two axes (77% combined), suggesting reward as PC1 and a hand-strength/board-match interaction as PC2.
conclusions.reward_metric_structure: Spearman rho=0.163 over pairwise distances — weaker than training-time diagnosis (0.641) due to stricter pairwise metric formulation and narrower state sample from random play.
conclusions.most_interesting_finding: Strong two-PC dominance (40.7% + 36.3%) with a sharp elbow in the scree plot. The encoder concentrated most information into two primary axes rather than distributing evenly across all 8.
conclusions.opponent_hand_footprint: Zero — confirms correct information masking; the encoder does not leak opponent private information into the embedding.
